question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What is the difference between players 6 and 23's jumping scores? difference between players 6 and 23's jumping scores refers to SUBTRACT(jumping AND id = 6,jumping AND id = 23)
orig_masked: What is the difference between [T5] [V2] and [V1]'s [C149]? difference between [T5] [V2] and [V1]'s [C149] refers to SUBTRACT([C149] AND [C147] = [V2],[C149] AND [C147] = [V1]); [V1] is a value of the column [C147]; [V2] is a value of the column [C147]
orig_evidence: difference between players 6 and 23's jumping scores refers to SUBTRACT(jumping AND id = 6,jumping AND id = 23)
orig_gold_sql: SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN t1.id = 6 THEN t1.jumping ELSE 0 END) - SUM(CASE WHEN t1.id = 23 THEN t1.jumping ELSE 0 END) FROM Player_Attributes AS t1

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: How many male customers who are living in North Bohemia have average salary greater than 8000? Male means that gender = 'M'; A3 refers to region; A11 pertains to average salary.
orig_masked: How many [C12] [T3] who are [C25] in [V1] have average [C18] greater than [V2]? [C12] means that [C12] = '[V3]'; [C25] refers to [C25]; [C18] pertains to average [C18].; [V1] is a value of the column [C25]; [V2] is a value of the column [C18]; [V3] is a value of the column [C12]
orig_evidence: Male means that gender = 'M'; A3 refers to region; A11 pertains to average salary.
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.client_id) FROM client AS T1 INNER JOIN district AS T2 ON T1.district_id = T2.district_id WHERE T1.gender = 'M' AND T2.A3 = 'north Bohemia' AND T2.A11 > 8000

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What was the chance creation crossing class for "Hull City" on 2010/2/22? "Hull City" refers to team_long_name = 'Hull City'; on 2010/2/22 refers to date = '2010-02-22 00:00:00'
orig_masked: What was the [C183] for "[V2]" on [V3]? "[V2]" refers to [C173] = '[V2]'; on [V3] refers to [C189] = '[V1]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C189]; [V2] is a value of the column [C173]; [V3] is a value of the column [C189]
orig_evidence: "Hull City" refers to team_long_name = 'Hull City'; on 2010/2/22 refers to date = '2010-02-22 00:00:00'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT t2.chanceCreationCrossingClass FROM Team AS t1 INNER JOIN Team_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.team_api_id = t2.team_api_id WHERE t1.team_long_name = 'Hull City' AND SUBSTR(t2.`date`, 1, 10) = '2010-02-22'

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Sort in descending order all patients by birthday for male patient with albumin not within range. male = SEX = 'M'; albumin not within range refers to ALB < = 3.5 or ALB > = 5.5
orig_masked: Sort in descending order all [T3] by [C59] for [C64] [T3] with [C14] not [C14]. [C64] = [C64] = '[V3]'; [C14] not [C14] refers to [C14] < = [V1] or [C14] > = [V2]; [V1] is a value of the column [C14]; [V2] is a value of the column [C14]; [V3] is a value of the column [C64]
orig_evidence: male = SEX = 'M'; albumin not within range refers to ALB < = 3.5 or ALB > = 5.5
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.ID FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.SEX = 'M' AND (T2.ALB <= 3.5 OR T2.ALB >= 5.5) ORDER BY T1.Birthday DESC

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Among the patients with a normal blood glucose, how many of them don't have thrombosis? normal blood glucose refers to GLU < 180; don't have thrombosis refers to Thrombosis = 0;
orig_masked: Among the [T3] with a [C27], how many of them don't have [C13]? [C27] refers to [C27] < [V1]; don't have [C13] refers to [C13] = [V2];; [V1] is a value of the column [C27]; [V2] is a value of the column [C13]
orig_evidence: normal blood glucose refers to GLU < 180; don't have thrombosis refers to Thrombosis = 0;
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID INNER JOIN Examination AS T3 ON T1.ID = T3.ID WHERE T2.GLU < 180 AND T3.Thrombosis = 0

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: What is the name of the superhero that has the most powers? name of the superhero refers to superhero_name; superhero that has the most powers refers to MAX(COUNT(superhero_name))
orig_masked: What is the [C28] of the [T9] that has the most [T6]? [C28] of the [T9] refers to [C28]; [T9] that has the most [T6] refers to MAX(COUNT([C28]))
orig_evidence: name of the superhero refers to superhero_name; superhero that has the most powers refers to MAX(COUNT(superhero_name))
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.superhero_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN hero_power AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.hero_id GROUP BY T1.superhero_name ORDER BY COUNT(T2.hero_id) DESC LIMIT 1

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Sum up the away team goal scored by both Daan Smith and Filipe Ferreira. Daan Smith refers to player_name = 'Daan Smith'; Filipe Ferreira refers to player_name = 'Filipe Ferreira'
orig_masked: Sum up the [C40] by both [V2] and [V1]. [V2] refers to [C126] = '[V2]'; [V1] refers to [C126] = '[V1]'; [V1] is a value of the column [C126]; [V2] is a value of the column [C126]
orig_evidence: Daan Smith refers to player_name = 'Daan Smith'; Filipe Ferreira refers to player_name = 'Filipe Ferreira'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT SUM(t2.away_team_goal) FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN match AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.away_player_5 WHERE t1.player_name IN ('Daan Smith', 'Filipe Ferreira')

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Identify superheroes who can control wind and list their names in alphabetical order.  superheroes refers to superhero_name; can control wind refers to power_name = 'Wind Control';
orig_masked: Identify [C28] who [C31] and list their [C28] in alphabetical order.  [C28] refers to [C28]; [C31] refers to [C31] = '[V1]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C31]
orig_evidence: superheroes refers to superhero_name; can control wind refers to power_name = 'Wind Control';
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.superhero_name FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN hero_power AS T2 ON T1.id = T2.hero_id INNER JOIN superpower AS T3 ON T2.power_id = T3.id WHERE T3.power_name = 'Wind Control' ORDER BY T1.superhero_name

question: <QUESTION>
orig_question: Name the ID of the patient who is born on the April 1st, 1982. Is his/her alkaliphophatase (ALP) within normal range? alkaliphophatase (ALP) within normal range refers to ALP < 300
orig_masked: Name the [C63] of the [T3] who is [C59] on the [V1]. Is his/her [C15]? [C15] refers to [C15] < [V2]; [V1] is a value of the column [C59]; [V2] is a value of the column [C15]
orig_evidence: alkaliphophatase (ALP) within normal range refers to ALP < 300
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.ID , CASE WHEN T2.ALP < 300 THEN 'normal' ELSE 'abNormal' END FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.Birthday = '1982-04-01'

question: <QUESTION>

orig_gold_sql: SELECT preferred_foot FROM Player_Attributes WHERE potential IS NOT NULL ORDER BY potential ASC LIMIT 1
